steps.title: Constant work rate produces a linear relationship
steps.content: The phrase constant rate is the shortcut. If the inspector checks the same number of shirts each unit of time, the number remaining changes by the same amount each unit of time, so the model is linear.

steps.title: Use a sample table to see the model type
steps.content: Pick any concrete constant-rate example, such as starting with $120$ shirts and checking $15$ shirts per hour. The points $(0,120)$, $(1,105)$, and $(2,90)$ fit a line with negative slope. direct calculation shows the same structure as the general model $\text{remaining}=N-rt$.

steps.title: Confirm the choice
steps.content: The number remaining decreases as time increases, so the slope is negative. A constant rate of decrease is a linear model with a negative slope, which is choice B.
